text:
**\
00:00**\
Theresa Anoje\
Hosting my own events.

**\
00:02**\
Vrijen Attawar\
Hey. Hey. How\'s it going? Sorry, I\'m just. I\'m moving around before I
go to the vet hospital to get my kitty, so I\'m just jumping in for a
little bit. But sorry for interrupting and. Hi, how\'s it going?

**\
00:13**\
Theresa Anoje\
Good to see you. Yes. So I\'m actually hosting some events for Tech Week
myself. I. Yes. I just started a group on Luma called Social Impact sf.
What I essentially do is I make a calendar where I aggregate events for
good all around the city. So that could be intent. For good. That can be
in nonprofit events, that could be political events. Right now the
calendar has over 100 events on it.

**\
00:37**\
Logan Currie\
Wow.

**\
00:38**\
Theresa Anoje\
Yes. And then I haven\'t even added Tech Week, so it\'s gonna just like,
balloon.

**\
00:42**\
Logan Currie\
Okay, that\'s so cool. Which, which when is your event?

**\
00:46**\
Theresa Anoje\
I\'m doing a mixer on October 7th. Let me get the link to it and you\'re
admitted. Let me see. Breathan, I don\'t know if you can hear me, but
are you planning to come to SF for Tech Week?

**\
00:57**\
Vrijen Attawar\
I am not, unfortunately. I am. I am still. No. What am I doing? No, I am
actually taking like a whole week off. If not, if not closer to eight,
nine days. Yeah, I\'m a little burnt out. So, you know, again, the lack
of responsiveness and stuff, a little bit down to me just being a little
bit low energy. But yeah, I. I\'m. I\'m taking a little bit of time off.
But we\'ll hopefully catch you in New York if you\'re in town next. I
think there\'s something after that, right? In New York.

**\
01:25**\
Theresa Anoje\
Absolutely. I\'m looking for excuses to come out to New York. I kind of
am bummed because it was just Climate Week apparently. And apparently
New York Climate Week is like the best one by far.

**\
01:34**\
Logan Currie\
Yeah.

**\
01:35**\
Vrijen Attawar\
Yeah. New York Climate Week is. Is absolutely great. My wife did some
events there. So it was. It was good times. Yeah, it was really. It was.
It was very cool to see the amount of, like, different industries that
now give a crap about the climate. Very.

**\
01:52**\
Theresa Anoje\
At least they\'re saying the right things.

**\
01:54**\
Vrijen Attawar\
They\'re saying right things. When you have finance bros doing lip
service, you really gives you hope in the world. Changing it really is
something. But yeah, you know, things have been good otherwise. Besides
killing it.

**\
02:09**\
Theresa Anoje\
Exactly. Yeah. Just very busy too. I don\'t know when I\'m going to fit
in a break, but it\'s my birthday on Wednesday. Yes, exactly. And I\'m
planning like a big night out the next day on Thursday with my friends,
just because they do this, like, monthly block party in San Francisco
called Downtown first Thursdays. So we\'re just going to be, like,
walking around the block party and then maybe finding, like, a bar for
afterward. And so I\'m looking forward to that. Yeah, a little bit of
stress relief, but I feel like I need, like, actual, like, once Tech
Week is done, I. I shouldn\'t move. Like, I should just, like,
hibernate.

**\
02:44**\
Vrijen Attawar\
I think, because literally, me both.

**\
02:47**\
Theresa Anoje\
Yes, it has, honestly.

**\
02:50**\
Vrijen Attawar\
Yeah. And I hope you get that too. I really do.

**\
02:53**\
Theresa Anoje\
Yes. Something that, like, you know, as I get more mentorship, as I
like, you know, like, get more guidance as a founder is, like, you do
feel this pressure to be very always on and. And I get, like, a ton
offers thrown at me. Like, for Tech Week, I had, like, four different
people approach me for events, and one event we moved to, like, after,
technically, because it\'s just like. No, it\'s too much on my plate.
One event, I. I said yes, and then I learned that this person doesn\'t
really, like, manage events super well, so I\'m, like, kind of myself.
And then another event is pretty straightforward. And then there\'s my
event. And so I am trying to figure out the smartest times to say no.

**\
03:35**\
Theresa Anoje\
I\'m so thankful I pushed one of the opportunities forward because it
gives us more time to, like, really make it great. I\'m actually
planning a hackathon.

**\
03:43**\
Logan Currie\
Cool.

**\
03:43**\
Theresa Anoje\
Yes. It\'s going to be a diversity and equity hackathon, and so that
we\'re hoping to do sometime in November.

**\
03:52**\
Vrijen Attawar\
Hey, do you want a connection with Major League Hacking? Are they on
your radar?

**\
03:57**\
Theresa Anoje\
I\'ve heard of Major League Hacking.

**\
03:58**\
Vrijen Attawar\
Yeah, I know the founder and we\'re actually chatting with them as well.
Lokes, you\'ve got a call with Major League Hacking Amanda. This Monday
or. No, sorry, this Wednesday.

**\
04:11**\
Logan Currie\
Wednesday, right.

**\
04:12**\
Vrijen Attawar\
Yeah, yeah, I\'ll inform her. Add that to the list. Yeah. So happy to
put you in touch once those plans are a little more baked in, if that is
helpful.

**\
04:24**\
Theresa Anoje\
Yes, thank you. Thank you. I appreciate that.

**\
04:27**\
Vrijen Attawar\
Yeah. Well, this is my awesome co founder, Logan. She\'s. When people.
When people say better half, they usually meet at a marriage context.
But funny, it applies all around.

**\
04:41**\
Logan Currie\
You have several. You have several better halves. Virgin. Your wife,
your wife, your mother, and me. Now.

**\
04:48**\
Theresa Anoje\
Yeah, yeah.

**\
04:50**\
Vrijen Attawar\
The through line is, you know, the through line and all of that is that
I\'m a man that knows to listen to good advice or at least, you know,
stubbornly can be dragged to it eventually.

**\
05:01**\
Logan Currie\
There you go.

**\
05:01**\
Theresa Anoje\
There you go.

**\
05:05**\
Logan Currie\
V Do you want to before you head off to get Gary? Gary is the cat.

**\
05:11**\
Vrijen Attawar\
I can. I was gonna. I was just gonna sort of jump on and sit in the back
of the car because I\'m taking a lift anyway.

**\
05:17**\
Logan Currie\
Okay, sure.

**\
05:18**\
Vrijen Attawar\
But I\'m happy to. But yeah, if you. If you think you can handle it
also, I\'m happy to just give you the space.

**\
05:24**\
Logan Currie\
I\'m happy to kind of take things from here. I have a kind of prep doc
from. From Ilsa, who unfortunately was not able to join Teresa. But I
can kind of like walk you through what I touch base with her on. If you
just want to maybe give me the. The two minute spiel of like, how y\'
all connected and then what you\'ve talked about so far, and then I can.
I can take things from there and then you can hop off and just go get
Gary.

**\
05:46**\
Theresa Anoje\
Does that work?

**\
05:46**\
Vrijen Attawar\
Yeah, yeah, absolutely. So I\'m just looking up the email that I sent
because that has a little bit more of that content. But yeah. Okay, so
long story short, the, you know, we met through. Through freaking Jess.
Yes.

**\
06:09**\
Theresa Anoje\
Okay.

**\
06:10**\
Vrijen Attawar\
Yes, we met Jess\'s group.

**\
06:12**\
Theresa Anoje\
Yes. Oh, I was just gonna explain who Jess is. So Jessica Greenwald, she
runs this, not this startup called Nice Industries. She started a track
for impact leaders. Jessica\'s was like one of those connector people.
Like, she gets around. She. She was in New York. She\'s based in
Seattle. She\'s like heavily connected to sf. Like, she\'s everywhere, I
think logs.

**\
06:34**\
Vrijen Attawar\
You met her at asu, gsv maybe?

**\
06:37**\
Logan Currie\
No, I\'ve never actually met her. We\'ve texted a thousand times trying
to meet up essentially, but we always miss each other like ships in the
night. But I\'ve heard amazing things about her. Yes. Oh, fantastic.
Next Tuesday. Okay, great. Wait, let me text her and say I\'m on the
phone with Teresa and I will be there. We will actually make it happen.
Okay, cool. Fantastic.

**\
07:02**\
Vrijen Attawar\
We keep going.

**\
07:03**\
Theresa Anoje\
Keep going.

**\
07:04**\
Vrijen Attawar\
Yeah. So we. We connected over that essentially sort of mutual interest
in helping people do more with their careers. And I think specifically
for Theresa, do more good through their careers. And so the thinking was
almost a. I think, Teresa, you have essentially spun up a community,
right? And you were thinking, how do I sort of monetize, how do I get
these folks, you know, thinking along the recruitment line? So I
essentially thought Teresa was like the perfect trial case for the,
like, ground up, built around career span, like virtual recruiter model.
Because I think to. To the point that you made a while back, Logan, I do
think that the real undiscovered niche is micro communities. You\'ve
been pretty sort of, like, sharp about that point.

**\
07:55**\
Vrijen Attawar\
And so I think Theresa\'s counts as one of those, like, budding
communities that we could use as a case study. And then, you know, for
our part, Theresa, I think we have tech that\'s ready to go. We have,
you know, employers that we\'re talking to, like almost a dozen at this
point and, you know, commensurate number of roles posted. So I think we
can start pushing stuff out to your folks. Yeah, it\'s just a matter of
plugging everything in, but logs can help guide that at that part of the
discussion. Yeah, sweet. Anything I missed or. And I\'ve sent over a
remotely good code with I think maybe like. I think I must have put like
100 licenses or 200 licenses on it. So we already have a code deployed
amongst a couple other things, but yeah. Theresa, did I miss anything?

**\
08:43**\
Theresa Anoje\
No, I mean, if I think of anything, you\'re going to be like, in the
background while you\'re running in a lift. Is that what you said?

**\
08:48**\
Vrijen Attawar\
Yeah, yeah, I\'ll be in the background. I just might be quiet, but I\'ll
be able to chat.

**\
08:52**\
Theresa Anoje\
Okay. I\'ll let you know if I think of anything, but that looks like the
gist.

**\
08:56**\
Logan Currie\
No, that\'s perfect.

**\
08:57**\
Vrijen Attawar\
Catch you guys in a bit. Bye.

**\
09:00**\
Logan Currie\
No worries.
